tex:
60 boys are asked to name their favourite sport.
\newline
The results are shown in the pie chart.
\newline

(a) Write down the most popular sport.
\newline

(b) Write down the fraction of boys who choose Running.
\newline

(c) Work out how many boys choose Judo.
\newline

(d) One of the boys is chosen at random.
\newline

Work out the probability that his favourite sport is not Judo.
\newline

(e) Complete this statement.
\newline

Three times as many boys choose  than choose 

Two of the boys in part (a) then change their choice from Running to Swimming.
\newline

Complete the pie chart after this change.
\newline
The Tennis, Judo and Hockey sectors have been drawn for you.
\newline

60 girls are asked to name their favourite sport.
\newline
Their results are shown in the bar chart below.
\newline

Using your pie chart in part (b) and the bar chart above, write down one similarity and one difference
between the girls' results and the boys' results.
\newline

Similarity 

Difference

    % Based on the context, this seems to be a rectangle with some shading
    \draw[thick,fill=gray!30] (0,0) -- (0,2) -- (1.71,2) -- (1.71,0) -- cycle; % approximately 3/7

    % Add a label or just leave as is since it's a simple rectangle
\end{tikzpicture}

qtype: ratio, percentage of an amount, operations with fractions, problem solving
tex:
A car park has 880 parking spaces.
\newline

(a) Some of the spaces are reserved.
\newline
The ratio of   reserved spaces : not reserved spaces = 1 : 10.
\newline

Work out the number of spaces that are not reserved.
\newline

(b) 25\% of the 880 spaces are on the top floor.
\newline

Work out the number of spaces that are on the top floor.
\newline

(c) At 06 00 one morning, $\dfrac{1}{10}$ of the 880 spaces are filled.
\newline
By 06 30, no cars have left the car park but another $\dfrac{1}{10}$ of the 880 spaces are filled.
\newline

Work out the fraction of the 880 spaces that are empty at 06 30.
\newline

(d) The cost of each visit to the car park is shown in the table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Length of visit & Cost (\$) \\
\hline
Up to 20 minutes & Free \\
\hline
More than 20 minutes and up to 2 hours & 2.50 \\
\hline
More than 2 hours and up to 4 hours & 4.50 \\
\hline
More than 4 hours and up to 8 hours & 8.50 \\
\hline
More than 8 hours and up to 24 hours & 12.00 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (e) Samarth arrives at 11 40 and leaves at 15 30.
\newline

Find the cost of his visit.
\newline

\$ 

(f) Radhika leaves the car park at 17 50 and pays \$8.50.
\newline

(a) Work out the earliest time she could have arrived at the car park.
\newline

(b) Work out the change she receives from a \$20 note.
\newline

\$ 

(g) Dhruv bought a weekly car park ticket for \$26.
\newline
That week, he visited the car park four times.
\newline
These are the lengths of time he parked his car for.
\newline

17 minutes   6$\frac{1}{2}$ hours   11 hours   9$\frac{1}{4}$ hours

Work out how much he saved by buying a weekly ticket.
\newline

\$

stem.content:
A car park has 880 parking spaces.
\newline

_rawBody:
A car park has 880 parking spaces.

\begin{parts}
\item Some of the spaces are reserved.
The ratio of   reserved spaces : not reserved spaces = 1 : 10.

Work out the number of spaces that are not reserved.

\AnswerLine[][]

\Marks{2}

\item 25\% of the 880 spaces are on the top floor.

Work out the number of spaces that are on the top floor.

\AnswerLine[][]

\Marks{1}

\item At 06 00 one morning, $\dfrac{1}{10}$ of the 880 spaces are filled.
By 06 30, no cars have left the car park but another $\dfrac{1}{10}$ of the 880 spaces are filled.

Work out the fraction of the 880 spaces that are empty at 06 30.

\AnswerLine[][]

\Marks{3}

\item The cost of each visit to the car park is shown in the table.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Length of visit & Cost (\$) \\
\hline
Up to 20 minutes & Free \\
\hline
More than 20 minutes and up to 2 hours & 2.50 \\
\hline
More than 2 hours and up to 4 hours & 4.50 \\
\hline
More than 4 hours and up to 8 hours & 8.50 \\
\hline
More than 8 hours and up to 24 hours & 12.00 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\begin{subparts}
\item Samarth arrives at 11 40 and leaves at 15 30.

Find the cost of his visit.

\$ \AnswerLine[]

\Marks{1}

\item Radhika leaves the car park at 17 50 and pays \$8.50.

(a) Work out the earliest time she could have arrived at the car park.

\AnswerLine[][]

\Marks{1}

(b) Work out the change she receives from a \$20 note.

\$ \AnswerLine[]

\Marks{1}

\item Dhruv bought a weekly car park ticket for \$26.
That week, he visited the car park four times.
These are the lengths of time he parked his car for.

17 minutes \quad 6$\frac{1}{2}$ hours \quad 11 hours \quad 9$\frac{1}{4}$ hours

Work out how much he saved by buying a weekly ticket.

\$ \AnswerLine[]

\Marks{3}
\end{subparts}
\end{parts}
